description_long: This object is a handheld electric drill with a robust, industrial design, primarily colored in military green with some black accents. The drill features a cylindrical body with a prominent, rounded motor housing at one end and a smaller, circular chuck at the other, which currently lacks a drill bit. A thick, black power cord is attached to the rear of the drill, looping around the body, indicating it is powered by mains electricity. The handle is integrated into the main body, and there are visible screws and seams along the casing, suggesting a durable, metallic or heavy-duty plastic construction. The surface appears hard and slightly matte, with some subtle texturing for grip. No brand logo or markings are visible, and the drill is equipped with ventilation slots on the motor housing for cooling.
description: A rugged, green electric drill with a black power cord, integrated handle, and visible ventilation slots, lacking a drill bit and brand markings.
description_view_0: The rear and side of the drill are visible, showing the power cord attachment and the squared motor housing.
description_view_1: A side profile highlights the length of the drill, the cylindrical shaft, and the power cord looping along the body.
description_view_2: The front of the drill is shown, focusing on the circular chuck where a drill bit would be attached.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the full length of the drill, the rounded motor housing, and the cord's path.
